Financial Stress Composite Signal
← Back to dashboard

Financial Stress Composite Signal

Systemic stress from credit spreads, curve inversion, and financial conditions.

As of 2036-10-01

Gemini Summary

Signal Summary:

  • Configuration statement (mandatory): Given the STLFSI4 and HY_OAS levels within the -0.75 to 0.75 neutral band, this setup aligns with Range-biased price paths and Normal volatility, where the dominant risk is Data uncertainty, not Regime shift.
  • The signal currently occupies a Neutral regime (1) with a composite value of -0.1257 as of March 2026.
  • Conviction Band: Medium; Interpretation Confidence: High Confidence; Internal Conflict Flag: No. Signal Stability Assessment: Volatile; Threshold Proximity: Moderate; Revision Sensitivity: Unknown.

Methodology Applied:

  • Composite values between -0.75 and 0.75 indicate Neutral conditions aligned with historical norms (1).
  • Rising values indicate a transition toward risk-off and economic slowdown (1).
  • The methodology integrates corporate credit risk premia (2) and broad market volatility (3).
  • Latest observation date: 2026-03-31.

Key Dynamics:

  • The primary driver is the rising Z-score of the St. Louis Fed Stress Index (Z_STL: 0.32), indicating building systemic pressure (3).
  • Credit spreads (HY_OAS at 3.17) remain a stabilizing force with a negative z-score (-0.16) (2).
  • The yield curve (T10Y2Y) provides a secondary offset to stress with a z-score of -0.54 (4).
  • Conditional Invalidation: Composite value exceeding 0.75, triggering a High Stress regime shift.
  • Signal stability is volatile; the composite has risen from -0.70 in July 2025 to -0.12 currently.

Scenario Balance:

  • Base case dominant: Continued stability within historical norms as sub-components remain balanced.
  • Upside risk: Transition to Low Stress triggered by further narrowing of HY spreads.
  • Downside risk: Shift toward High Stress triggered by a spike in STLFSI4 or yield curve inversion.

Time Horizon & Aggregation:

  • Time Horizon: Cyclical (months) due to the signal’s monthly frequency and persistence of regime bands.
  • Aggregation Weight Hint: Medium, as it serves as a broad gauge of funding and risk appetite.

Macro Relevance:

  • Informs the liquidity and pricing dimensions of the macro environment.
  • Economic mechanism: Directional shifts imply changes in funding liquidity and corporate credit availability.
  • Cycle position: not determined.
  • Typically interacts with volatility and credit signals to confirm regime transitions.

Regime Context:

  • Persistent Neutral regime since April 2023.
  • Direction of change: Strengthening (rising stress levels within the neutral band).

Model Limitations:

  • Monthly frequency misses intra-month spikes.
  • Lagging nature of yield curve inversion.

Data & References:

  • Financial Stress Composite (2026-03-31), HY OAS (2026-03-31), T10Y2Y (2026-03-31).
  • The St. Louis Fed Stress Index (STLFSI4) is the most influential driver of recent momentum.
  • Public datasets: VIX and TED Spread would improve sub-component granularity.

Composite of STLFSI, HY spreads, and yield-curve inversion.
